title: chaenorhinum crassifolium subsp. cadevallii
intro: Chaenorhinum crassifolium subsp. cadevallii, also known as Cadevall's Chaenorhinum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties due to its high content of essential oils, including borneol and borneol acetate, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Chaenorhinum crassifolium subsp. cadevallii include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions or decoctions to make teas, tinctures, and salves that can be applied topically to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
